fixes_applied.original_issue: rm -rf block-list bypass: string-replacement approach allowed bypass via separated flags (rm -f -r /), reversed flags, quoted paths, and only checked 4 critical paths (/, ~, /*, ~/*)
fixes_applied.fix_applied: Replaced entire string-replacement check with POSIX tokeniser: collect_flags() accumulates all single-char flags from combined (-rf) and separated (-r -f) tokens, has_recursive_force() checks for r+f flags in any combination, extract_rm_paths() strips wrapping quotes and rejects tokens containing shell metacharacters ($;|&`), path_is_critical() normalises the path and checks against expanded CRITICAL_PATH_PREFIXES (17 paths: /, ~, /home, /root, /etc, /usr, /var, /boot, /sys, /proc, /dev, /lib, /lib64, /bin, /sbin, /opt and wildcards). Also rewrote has_pipe_to_shell() to detect any known shell binary by name (sh, bash, zsh, fish, dash, ksh, csh, tcsh, ash) after a pipe, including /bin/sh paths. Updated check_high_risk_patterns() to use the same collect_flags/has_recursive_force logic. Added 14 new security regression tests covering all bypass classes.

fixes_applied.original_issue: ToolCall cloned unconditionally on every tool execution even when no name repair is needed, allocating new Strings and deep-copying the JSON arguments Value unnecessarily
fixes_applied.fix_applied: Changed execute_tool_call_timed() to call repair_tool_name() first, then only allocate an owned ToolCall (with clone) when the name actually changed. When no repair is needed, uses the original &ToolCall directly with no allocation. The second clone (passed to self.tools.execute()) is now the only unavoidable copy.

fixes_applied.original_issue: build_system_prompt_suffix() performs blocking file I/O (fs::canonicalize, fs::read_dir, fs::read_to_string across ~10 call sites) and is called from an async context while an RwLock guard may be held
fixes_applied.fix_applied: Added build_system_prompt_suffix_async() that wraps the synchronous implementation in tokio::task::spawn_blocking. The async version takes owned parameters so no lock is held across the await point.

fixes_applied.original_issue: Called blocking build_system_prompt_suffix() directly in async run() after releasing the mode_prompt_suffix RwLock
fixes_applied.fix_applied: Changed call site to use build_system_prompt_suffix_async() with .await, passing mode_suffix and model_name as owned Strings and cfg.instructions cloned before the await point so no locks are held across it.

fixes_applied.original_issue: DEFAULT_MAX_RETRIES was a private const; all five providers passed hardcoded literal 3 to send_with_retry_cb() instead of referencing the constant
fixes_applied.fix_applied: Changed DEFAULT_MAX_RETRIES to pub const. Updated all five providers (openai.rs, anthropic.rs, gemini.rs, copilot.rs, openrouter.rs) to pass common::DEFAULT_MAX_RETRIES instead of the literal 3.

fixes_applied.original_issue: default_provider() hardcodes magic literals 5 (failure threshold) and 30 (cooldown secs) with no named constants
fixes_applied.fix_applied: Added pub const DEFAULT_FAILURE_THRESHOLD: u32 = 5 and pub const DEFAULT_COOLDOWN_SECS: u64 = 30 at module level with doc comments. Updated default_provider() to use these constants.

fixes_applied.original_issue: Five consecutive eprintln! calls for server startup status bypass structured logging and are invisible to log collectors
fixes_applied.fix_applied: Replaced all eprintln! calls with tracing::info! calls to route through the configured subscriber consistently with the rest of the application.

fixes_applied.original_issue: Magic number 2000 (chars) for memory block truncation hardcoded in two independent crates (ava-agent and ava-praxis) with no shared definition
fixes_applied.fix_applied: Added pub const MEMORY_BLOCK_MAX_CHARS: usize = 2000 to ava-types/src/lib.rs with a doc comment explaining the rationale. Updated both usage sites to import and use the constant.

fixes_applied.original_issue: Hardcoded literal 2000 for memory block truncation
Duplicate hardcoded literal 2000 for worker result truncation, independent of the same limit in ava-agent
Magic divisor 3 (instruction_budget = context_window / 3) with no named constant or documented rationale
fixes_applied.fix_applied: Added const INSTRUCTION_BUDGET_DIVISOR: usize = 3 with a doc comment explaining the 1/3 allocation rationale. Updated the budget calculation to use it.

fixes_applied.original_issue: Three fire-and-forget tokio::spawn calls for set_thinking_level, cycle_thinking, and set_mode silently discard errors
fixes_applied.fix_applied: Deferred. The underlying AgentStack methods (set_thinking_level, set_mode_prompt_suffix, set_plan_mode) return () not Result, so there is nothing to log without changing their signatures. Adding error logging requires changing those function signatures to return Result, which is a larger cross-crate refactor.
fixes_applied.reason_if_deferred: AgentStack methods return () not Result; adding error surfacing requires a multi-crate signature change that would be a separate PR-scope refactor.

fixes_applied.original_issue: Codebase indexing tokio::spawn drops its JoinHandle; if the task panics, the index is silently never built
fixes_applied.fix_applied: Deferred. The task already logs via warn!() on Err. Retaining the JoinHandle requires adding a field to AgentStack and plumbing it through to the first codebase_search call — architectural change beyond targeted fix scope.
fixes_applied.reason_if_deferred: Requires structural change to AgentStack to hold JoinHandle and poll it lazily. The existing Err path already emits a tracing::warn. Deferring to a dedicated async hygiene sprint.

summary: Fixed 3 critical issues (rm -rf bypass with 6 bypass classes covered + 14 new regression tests, unconditional ToolCall clone, blocking fs in async context) and 8 warning-level issues (DEFAULT_MAX_RETRIES constant propagated to 5 providers, circuit breaker magic numbers extracted to constants, web server eprintln replaced with tracing, MEMORY_BLOCK_MAX_CHARS constant shared across 2 crates, instruction budget divisor named). 2 findings deferred (fire-and-forget spawn error logging and codebase index JoinHandle retention) because they require multi-crate signature changes. All workspace tests pass (0 failures).
